content.problemStatement:
Find the number of sequences with KKK elements which satisfy the following conditions:


Each element is an integer between LLL and RRR inclusive.
The bitwise XOR of all elements is between AAA and BBB inclusive.
Since the number of sequences satisfying the given conditions can be quite large, compute it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: In the first testcase, we have to choose one integer in [26,54]∩[18,34]=[26,34][26, 54] \cap [18, 34] = [26, 34][26,54]∩[18,34]=[26,34]. So, there are 9 ways.
In the second testcase, we have to choose two integers in [3,5][3, 5][3,5] with bitwise xor in [4,7][4, 7][4,7]. There are 4 possible ways:
In the third testcase all 838^383 possible sequences are valid, hence the answer is 512512512.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains five space-separated integers LLL, RRR, AAA, BBB and KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of valid sequences modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
